title: Tangential
summary: Defines weighted multi-tangentiality: replacing any input by a boundary-equivalent representative does not change the restricted output.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines weighted multi-tangentiality: replacing any input by a boundary-equivalent representative does not change the restricted output.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/Tangential.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Rank-r tangentiality at a multiweight. Each input slot carries its own weight and its own weighted boundary equivalence relation. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, p of type cal conf.multi operator r, and w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, its defining expression is for every F G : Cal Conf.Multi Input r, Cal Conf.Is Multi Homogeneous w F maps to Cal Conf.Is Multi Homogeneous w G maps to Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value At Weights w F G maps to Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value P F P G.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights}(P,w)=\forall {F G \colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r}, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsMultiHomogeneous} w F \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsMultiHomogeneous} w G \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValueAtWeights} w F G \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} (P F) (P G)
correspondence.leanType: def IsTangentialAtWeights {r : ℕ} (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A rank-r operator maps a multiweight to an output weight. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, p of type cal conf.multi operator r, w of type fin r maps to the real numbers, and wout in the real numbers, its defining expression is for every F : Cal Conf.Multi Input r, Cal Conf.Is Multi Homogeneous w F maps to Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous wout P F.
correspondence.leanType: def MapsWeights {r : ℕ} (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (wout : ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A weighted rank-r tangential ambient operator. Its mathematical data are: input Weights: Fin r maps to the real numbers; output Weight: the real numbers; to Operator: Cal Conf.Multi Operator r; maps Weights: Cal Conf.Maps Weights to Operator input Weights output weight; tangential: Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weights to Operator input Weights.
Weighted bidifferential ambient operators. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Weighted Multi Tangential Operator 2.
Weighted tridifferential ambient operators. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Weighted Multi Tangential Operator 3.

For r in the natural numbers, P, Q of type Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, w of type Fin r maps to the real numbers, hP of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weights P w, and hQ of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weights Q w, Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weights Cal Conf.multi Add P Q w.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;P,Q\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;hP\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} P w,\;hQ\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} Q w,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{multiAdd} P Q) w
correspondence.leanType: theorem add {r : ℕ} {P Q : CalConf.MultiOperator r} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (hP : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights P w) (hQ : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights Q w) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (CalConf.multiAdd P Q) w

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, P of type Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, w of type Fin r maps to the real numbers, c in the real numbers, and hP of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weights P w, Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weights Cal Conf.multi Smul c P w.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;P\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;c\colon \mathbb{R},\;hP\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} P w,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{multiSmul} c P) w
correspondence.leanType: theorem smul {r : ℕ} {P : CalConf.MultiOperator r} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (c : ℝ) (hP : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights P w) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (CalConf.multiSmul c P) w

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Add weighted tangential operators with the same input and output weights. The output homogeneity proof is supplied explicitly; proving it from X linearity belongs to a later algebraic layer.
correspondence.leanType: def add {r : ℕ} (P Q : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r) (hinput : P.inputWeights = Q.inputWeights) (_houtput : P.outputWeight = Q.outputWeight) (hmaps : CalConf.MapsWeights (CalConf.multiAdd P.toOperator Q.toOperator) P.inputWeights P.outputWeight) : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Scalar multiples of weighted tangential operators. The output homogeneity proof is supplied explicitly; proving it from X linearity belongs to a later algebraic layer. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, c in the real numbers, p of type cal conf.weighted multi tangential operator r, and hmaps of type cal conf.maps weights cal conf.multi smul c p.to operator p.input weights p.output weight, its defining expression is input Weights : equals P.input Weights output weight : equals P.output weight to Operator : equals Cal Conf.multi Smul c P.to Operator maps Weights : equals hmaps tangential : equals Is Tangential At Weights.smul c P.tangential.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{smul}(c,P,hmaps)=\operatorname{inputWeights} = P.\operatorname{inputWeights} w_{\mathrm{out}} = P.\operatorname{outputWeight} \operatorname{toOperator} = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{multiSmul} c P.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{mapsWeights} = \operatorname{hmaps} \operatorname{tangential} = \operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights}.\operatorname{smul} c P.\operatorname{tangential}
correspondence.leanType: def smul {r : ℕ} (c : ℝ) (P : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r) (hmaps : CalConf.MapsWeights (CalConf.multiSmul c P.toOperator) P.inputWeights P.outputWeight) : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
